issues.title: Update CLAUDE.md to document reviewer degradation behaviour
issues.body_preview: Update `CLAUDE.md` to reflect the new fallback behaviour: the review chain section should note that `reviewWithLLM()` retries transient API errors (via `withRetry` in `claude-client.ts`) before falling back to rule-based `reviewOutput()`, and that degradation is surfaced to the `IOAdapter` via `send

issues.title: Integration test: reviewer degradation fallback path
issues.body_preview: Add a test case to `src/integration/pipeline.integration.test.ts` (e.g. "13. Reviewer Degradation"). Configure `MockLLMClient` to throw a network-style error on reviewer calls for the orchestrator agent. Verify that: (a) the pipeline completes successfully using rule-based fallback, (b) the `TestAda

issues.title: Surface reviewer degradation to IOAdapter in `reviewed.ts`
issues.body_preview: In `src/agents/reviewed.ts`, check the `degraded` flag on the `ReviewResult` returned by `reviewWithLLM()`. When `degraded` is true and an `adapter` is available in `ReviewOptions`, call `adapter.sendStatus("⚠ LLM reviewer unavailable — using rule-based fallback")` so the user sees a degradation not

issues.title: Add `degraded` flag to `ReviewResult` type and set it in `llm-reviewer.ts` fallback
issues.body_preview: Add an optional `degraded?: boolean` field to the `ReviewResult` type in `src/types.ts`. In `src/agents/llm-reviewer.ts`, set `degraded: true` on the `ReviewResult` returned from the catch block (the rule-based fallback path). Replace the bare `console.error` in the fallback with a `verbose()` struc

issues.title: Unit tests for `withRetry` in `claude-client.test.ts`
issues.body_preview: Create `src/agents/claude-client.test.ts`. Mock the Anthropic SDK to throw `APIStatusError(429)` twice then succeed, and verify that `sendMessage` retries the expected number of times and ultimately returns the successful response. Add a test that non-retryable errors (e.g. status 400) are thrown im

issues.title: Add `withRetry` helper to `claude-client.ts` for 429/503/network errors
issues.body_preview: Implement a `withRetry<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>, maxAttempts?: number): Promise<T>` helper inside `src/agents/claude-client.ts` that catches `Anthropic.APIStatusError` with status 429 or 503, and network-level fetch failures, then retries with exponential backoff (2 s, 4 s, 8 s) before rethrowing. No

issues.title: Initiative system: InitiativeBuilder agent
issues.body_preview:
## What
An InitiativeBuilder agent that receives a user request identified by the Orchestrator as initiative-worthy (recurring pattern, time-bounded goal) and creates a persisted Initiative entry — including cron expression, architecture type, stop condition, and evidence links back to user statemen

issues.title: Initiative Table & Persistence
issues.body_preview:
A persistent table for Initiatives, separate from the Job table. Initiatives own *why* and *when*; Jobs own *what* and *how*. When an Initiative's cron fires, it creates (or re-runs) a Job — the Initiative itself doesn't execute work directly.

## Why
The Job Graph provides execution. But recurring,

issues.title: User Statement Log
issues.body_preview:
A persistent, append-only log of user statements the system considers *evidence* for work. Provides an N:N join between Jobs and Statements, enabling provenance queries, drift detection, and conflict detection across Initiatives.

## Why
Jobs and Initiatives need traceable justification. When the sy

issues.title: Tech debt: implement request-modifications reviewer decision (re-call loop)
issues.body_preview:
## What
The `request-modifications` reviewer decision is a defined output type in `ReviewInterface.md` and the `ReviewDecision` type, but `applyReview()` in `reviewed.ts` currently treats it as a halt rather than looping back to the subject agent with the requested changes.

## Why
Without the re-ca

issues.title: Tech debt: add error recovery for LLM API failures (rate limits, network)
issues.body_preview:
## What
LLM API call failures (rate limit 429, transient network errors, timeouts) currently surface as unhandled exceptions and kill the in-progress request. The system needs a basic retry/backoff strategy and graceful degradation (e.g., fall back to rule-based reviewer on transient LLM reviewer fa

issues.title: Tech debt: upgrade to Node 20+ (vitest 4.x engine warning)
issues.body_preview:
## What
Vitest 4.x emits an engine compatibility warning because the project specifies Node 18 in `package.json`. Upgrading to Node 20 (current LTS) resolves the warning and keeps the test runner happy.

## Why
The warning clutters `npm test` output and signals drift from an EOL Node version. Node 2

issues.title: Model management: route requests to cheapest competent model per task
issues.body_preview:
## What
A model router that selects the cheapest capable model for each agent invocation based on task type. Orchestrator/Reviewer tasks need strong reasoning; Planner/Executor/DW tasks need structured JSON output. The router maps agent role → appropriate model tier, with per-agent overrides availab

issues.title: Web Dashboard
issues.body_preview:
A browser-based UI served from within the AgentOS container (or as a local HTTP server for development). Provides human visibility into the running system and a surface for resolving FAFC confirmations, without requiring terminal access.

## Why
As the system grows more autonomous, the human needs l

issues.title: Adjutant: maintenance agent
issues.body_preview:
## What
The Adjutant is the system's maintenance and self-management agent. It is an OS-class agent with its own `adjutant-agent.md` config, whose tasks are modeled as system-class Initiatives that fire on cron schedules. Maintenance work (log rotation, job store pruning, staging cleanup, config coh

issues.title: Scenario CI via Claude Code Web
issues.body_preview: Use Claude Code web sessions as an automated integration testing environment. Each session boots a clean Writ instance, feeds it behavioral scenarios, observes results, and validates that the system performs as expected — including script authoring, review, and execution. Doubles as a self-improveme

issues.title: Multi-source command input
issues.body_preview:
Support an array of input sources rather than a single command channel, with the ability to add, revoke, and assign labels to sources at runtime.

## Why
The current Gist command channel uses a single `WRIT_COMMAND_GIST_ID` in `.env`. This works for a single operator but has three concrete limitatio

issues.title: Developer/Writer Branch-Based Script Staging
issues.body_preview:
Replace the flat `runtime/staging/scripts/` directory with a git branch workflow: each DW session creates a feature branch (keyed by GUID), scripts are committed there, and promotion to live means merging to main and pulling into `src/instance/scripts/`.

## Why
The current staging model (write to t

issues.title: BIG_BROTHER Proactive Config Optimization
issues.body_preview:
A proactive mode for BIG_BROTHER that analyzes accumulated execution logs and proposes agent config improvements based on observed patterns — not just in response to Reviewer-Reviewer flags.

## Why
The reactive path (RR flag → BB fix) addresses individual violations. But systemic drift — agents tha

issues.title: ClawdBot Integration & Untrusted Execution
issues.body_preview:
A potential sandbox model for imported ClawdBot skills and agents that lack Writ-native review guarantees. Also covers a vetting pipeline to transform imported skills into proper Writ scripts or agents.

## Why
ClawdBot and similar systems may offer useful capabilities that would be expensive to rep

issues.title: Script Branch Workflow + Code-Reviewing-Agent
issues.body_preview:
Scripts are developed on branches and merged to main only after a PR review. A Code-Reviewing-Agent participates in the review before merge, providing quality and correctness assessment. Formalizes script promotion with an accountable audit trail accessible via terminal and Gitea web UI.

## Why
Onc

issues.title: Gitea Integration
issues.body_preview:
Gitea embedded in the AgentOS container, hosting the script repository. A port is exposed so the human operator can inspect scripts, history, and PRs via browser without SSH access. Agents interact with Gitea via its API for branch and PR operations.

## Why
Scripts are the system's growing capabili

issues.title: Containerization
issues.body_preview:
AgentOS ships as a self-contained deployable container. All internal services — Gitea, script runner, logs, dashboard — run inside it. A user deploys one container and gets a fully functional instance.

## Why
The "agent as a self-expanding OS" analogy implies a deployable unit, not a manual install

issues.title: Throbber / Acknowledgment UX
issues.body_preview:
Per-channel activity indicators and acknowledgment patterns for asynchronous job execution. Each IOAdapter implementation is responsible for its own throbber/spinner; the Orchestrator provides the timing contract.

## Why
When a job takes longer than expected, the user needs feedback: either a visua

issues.title: Script intake pipeline for third-party scripts
issues.body_preview:
When Writ ingests a script from an external source (downloaded, user-provided, community library), it should pass through a review pipeline before promotion to `src/instance/scripts/`.

**Proposed flow:**
1. **Download/receive** script into a quarantine directory (e.g., `runtime/staging/intake/`)
2.

issues.title: Concurrent execution guard: lockfile or queue for Gist poll + pipeline overlap
issues.body_preview:
## Context

Cron fires on a fixed schedule. If a previous command is still being processed when the next poll runs, there is no guard preventing overlap.

## Problem

- Cron fires → poll script writes `A.cmd` to inbox
- Pipeline starts processing `A.cmd`
- Cron fires again → poll script writes `B.cm

issues.title: Self-scheduled polling: replace bootstrap cron with Writ-managed job
issues.body_preview:
## Context

The Gist command channel currently relies on a bootstrap cron job to poll for incoming commands (see `docs/deployment/gist-command-channel.md`). This is a deliberate temporary measure.

## What needs to happen

Writ should eventually schedule its own polling via its internal job/initiati

issues.title: Script discovery for agents: registry or convention for available scripts
issues.body_preview:
## Context

Agents need a way to know what scripts are available and what they do. Currently there is no registry or discovery convention for this.

## Problem

When an agent (e.g., Lieutenant Planner) needs to delegate work to a shell script, it has no reliable way to enumerate available scripts, u

issues.title: IOAdapter inbox integration: read .cmd files from runtime/inbox/ into pipeline
issues.body_preview:
## Context

The Gist command channel (see `docs/deployment/gist-command-channel.md`) writes plaintext commands as `.cmd` files to `runtime/inbox/`. This issue tracks wiring that inbox into the Writ pipeline.

## What needs to happen

- `IOAdapter` (or a new `GistAdapter`) should watch/read `.cmd` fi

issues.title: System health assessment: proactive agent config coherence
issues.body_preview:
## What
A periodic assessment that checks all agent configs (XYZ-AGENT.md files) for coherence against CONSTITUTION.md and SOUL.md, outside of RR flag triggers. When drift is detected — configs that contradict core values, are internally inconsistent, or reference deprecated patterns — the system fl

issues.title: Prompt evolution: A/B testing of agent prompts
issues.body_preview:
## What
A framework for A/B testing changes to agent prompts (XYZ-AGENT.md files) with tracked performance metrics. BIG_BROTHER proposes a prompt change → the system runs both variants for N requests → metrics are compared → the winning variant is promoted. All of this goes through the existing revi

issues.title: Get-to-know-the-user: proactive onboarding and USER.md
issues.body_preview:
## What
A proactive onboarding loop that builds a persistent `USER.md` file capturing what the system has learned about the user: their preferences, working style, recurring goals, and known system shortcomings relevant to them. Writ periodically updates USER.md through conversation and discloses kn

issues.title: Gist-based command channel
issues.body_preview:
## What
A pull-based command channel using a private GitHub Gist as a dead drop. A cron-driven poll script fetches the Gist, writes commands as `.cmd` files to `runtime/inbox/`, and a post script writes responses back. The IOAdapter integration reads inbox files and feeds them into `handleRequest()`
